---
title: "QED Labs"
page-layout: full
---

QED Labs is organized around three working sections:

- [Notes](notes/index.qmd) for method overviews and source-based reading notes
- [Reports](reports/index.qmd) for longer evaluator-facing report drafts
- [Labs](labs/index.qmd) for worksheet-style design exercises

## Site Map

This network map shows internal pages in blue and linked external sources in orange.

```{=html}
<div id="link-graph-shell" class="link-graph-shell">
  <div class="link-graph-controls" role="group" aria-label="Link graph controls">
    <label class="link-graph-search" for="link-graph-search">
      <span class="link-graph-control-label">Find node</span>
      <input id="link-graph-search" type="search" placeholder="Search by label or path" list="link-graph-node-options" />
    </label>
    <label class="link-graph-toggle" for="toggle-internal">
      <input id="toggle-internal" type="checkbox" checked />
      <span>Internal nodes</span>
    </label>
    <label class="link-graph-toggle" for="toggle-external">
      <input id="toggle-external" type="checkbox" checked />
      <span>External nodes</span>
    </label>
    <button id="reset-link-graph" class="link-graph-button link-graph-button-secondary" type="button">Reset view</button>
    <button id="toggle-fullscreen" class="link-graph-button" type="button" aria-pressed="false">Expand visual</button>
    <datalist id="link-graph-node-options"></datalist>
  </div>
  <div class="link-graph-legend">
    <span class="link-graph-legend-item link-graph-legend-item-internal"><span class="kind-pill internal">internal</span> QED Labs pages</span>
    <span class="link-graph-legend-item link-graph-legend-item-external"><span class="kind-pill external">external</span> Linked external sources</span>
    <span class="link-graph-legend-note">Click a node for details, drag to reposition, and scroll to zoom.</span>
  </div>
  <div id="link-graph-root" class="link-graph-root">
    <div class="link-graph-canvas-wrap">
      <div id="link-graph-svg" class="link-graph-svg" role="img" aria-label="Interactive QED Labs site map"></div>
    </div>
    <aside id="link-graph-details" class="link-graph-details">
      <h3>Node Details</h3>
      <p>Select a node to inspect its links.</p>
    </aside>
  </div>
</div>

<script src="assets/vendor/d3.min.js"></script>
<script src="data/link_graph_payload.js"></script>
<script src="assets/js/link_network.js"></script>
```

## Current Emphasis

The strongest current coverage is in matching and weighting, where the site now combines:

- a notes section with both overview and source-specific reading notes
- a report draft that can be expanded into a full matching writeup
- a migrated lab set built from the earlier `matching-trg` material

Synthetic control now has:

- a notes section
- a report skeleton draft
- two design-lab skeletons for future build-out

## Working Rule

Across all three sections, the standard is the same: define the design first, document diagnostics clearly, and separate what the design supports from what the analyst would like it to say.
